title: Krishi Vidya Nidhi Yojana (Odisha)
benefits: Course-specific scholarship amounts for private institutions: B.Tech Engineering ₹60000 per year for 4 years, M.Tech Engineering ₹66000 per year for 2 years, MBA ₹65000 per year, MBBS 4-5 years ₹37950 per year, Medical PG 2 years ₹58000 per year, B.Pharma 4 years ₹35000 per year, Nursing ₹19450 per year, Architecture ₹60000 per year, Government institutions receive actual course fees without cap, Hostel and mess charges ₹1200 per month for 10 months for UG PG PhD students totaling ₹12000 per year, Hostel and mess charges ₹1000 per month for 10 months for Diploma Polytechnic ITI students totaling ₹10000 per year, Covers tuition fees annual fees one-time fees and accommodation costs, Direct Benefit Transfer to student Aadhaar-linked bank account, Scholarship continues till course completion, Benefits 13923 students in 2024-25 with ₹53.57 crore disbursed including 55 students from NIT and IIM

step_guide: 1. Visit scholarship.odisha.gov.in and click Login, 2. Click Student Login option, 3. For new users click Register Here or visit registration guideline page, 4. Read instructions tick declaration checkbox and click Proceed, 5. Enter Aadhaar number and click Get OTP, 6. Validate OTP received on Aadhaar-registered mobile number, 7. Complete registration form with basic details and create password, 8. Login with unique number or Aadhaar number and password, 9. Navigate to Schemes section and select Krishi Vidya Nidhi Yojana, 10. Click Apply Now button, 11. Fill application form with personal academic bank and CM-KISAN beneficiary details, 12. Upload all required documents including CM-KISAN verification, 13. Complete Aadhaar-based face authentication, 14. Submit application and download receipt, 15. Institution will verify application online, 16. Track status through applicant dashboard
selection: Merit-based selection for permanent residents of Odisha whose parents or guardians are registered beneficiaries under CM-KISAN Scheme. Students must secure admission in government or private institutions having AISHE code within Odisha state. Cannot receive other scholarship benefits simultaneously must opt out from existing scholarships to receive KVNY benefits. Scholarship applicable for one professional course only students who received benefit for one program not eligible for second course. Private institutions located outside Odisha not eligible. Application window opens twice yearly in July-August and November-December. Institution principal must verify and authenticate application online before final approval by Agriculture and Farmers Empowerment Department.
intro_seo: The Krishi Vidya Nidhi Yojana provides financial assistance to children of CM-KISAN beneficiaries in Odisha for professional and technical courses with course-specific amounts ranging from ₹19450 to ₹66000 per year plus hostel allowance covering tuition annual fees and accommodation costs till course completion.

title: Green Passage Scheme - Higher Education (Odisha)
benefits: Complete fee waiver for all educational expenses including tuition fees admission and readmission fees examination fees hostel admission fees laboratory fees library fees college development fees and all other institutional charges, Free accommodation and food provided in government institutions where available, Reimbursement up to ₹5,00,000 for complete course duration in non-government unaided private institutions, In government and aided colleges fees exempted at admission with government reimbursing institution directly, In unaided private institutions students pay upfront and claim reimbursement later with maximum cap ₹5 lakh for entire course, Covers COVID-19 orphans who lost single or both parents during pandemic, No restriction on course type - all general professional technical courses eligible, Support continues for entire course duration till completion
docs_needed:
Aadhaar card
Death certificate of parent or parents
Residential certificate proving Odisha domicile
Income certificate from Tahasildar showing guardian or surviving parent income below ₹8 lakh
Bank passbook copy with account number and IFSC
Enrollment proof or admission letter from institution
Previous educational qualification certificates
Recent passport size photograph
Specially Cared Child Certificate or I-Card if COVID-19 orphan
DCPO certification if child was in Child Care Institution
step_guide: 1. Download application form from https://dhe.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2021-06/GREEN PASSAGE-4-10.pdf, 2. Fill application form completely with all required details, 3. Gather all required documents including death certificate income certificate and enrollment proof, 4. If child was in Child Care Institution obtain certification from DCPO District Child Protection Officer, 5. If COVID-19 orphan obtain Specially Cared Child Certificate or I-Card, 6. Submit completed application with documents to DCPO or Child Welfare Committee offline, 7. DCPO verifies documents and forwards to Higher Education Department, 8. Department prepares merit list based on academic performance and family income, 9. Results announced publicly by department, 10. For government and aided colleges fees exempted at admission, 11. For unaided private colleges students pay fees and apply for reimbursement with fee receipts and vouchers, 12. Reimbursement transferred to student Aadhaar-linked bank account via DBT
selection: Merit-based selection for orphaned and vulnerable students who are permanent residents of Odisha. Eligible categories include: child without biological or adoptive parents, child who lost both parents, child who lost single earning parent, child residing in Child Care Institution foster care or under guardian with annual income below ₹8 lakh. Scheme extended in 2020 to cover children who lost single or both parents due to COVID-19 pandemic. Must be enrolled in higher education meaning any general professional or technical course of at least 1 year duration after Class 12 in recognized government or non-government institution college or university within Odisha. Girl students rescued from child marriage by Women and Child Development Department staying in shelter home or hostel eligible till age 21 or marriage whichever occurs first. Selection based on verification of orphan status through death certificates and DCPO certification combined with academic merit and income criteria.
helpline: Higher Education Department Government of Odisha, Bhubaneswar-751001

title: Shahid Madho Singh Haath Kharcha Yojana (Odisha)
benefits: One-time financial assistance of ₹5,000 at time of admission into Class 9 or Class 11, Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account after school verification, Aims to reduce dropout rate among ST students after Class 8 and Class 10, Can be availed along with other scholarship schemes including Post-Matric ST scholarship, Targeted towards 3 lakh ST students across Odisha, Named after tribal freedom fighter Shaheed Madho Singh, Scheme launched on martyrdom day (December 31, 2024) of Shaheed Madho Singh, Covers both boys and girls from ST community, Benefits available for students in government and government-aided schools only, Payment made after Principal/Head of Institution recommendation and verification
docs_needed:
Aadhaar card
ST Caste certificate issued by competent authority
Income certificate from Tahasildar (family income from all sources)

step_guide: 1. Application is NOT submitted by students directly - Process handled through school Principal/Head Master, 2. Students must inform school Principal about their eligibility for the scheme, 3. Principal/Head of Institution logs into State Scholarship Portal at https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in/, 4. Principal fills online application on behalf of eligible ST students, 5. Required student details entered: Personal info, academic details, bank account, Aadhaar number, 6. Principal uploads all required documents digitally, 7. Principal validates and recommends the application online, 8. Application submitted by school authority before deadline, 9. ST & SC Development Department verifies applications at district level, 10. Eligible students receive ₹5,000 via DBT to their bank account, 11. Students can track status through scholarship portal using application number
selection: Only for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students who are bonafide residents of Odisha. Student must have completed Class 8 and newly admitted to Class 9 in same academic year, OR completed Class 10 and newly admitted to Class 11 in same academic year. This is a ONE-TIME incentive given at time of first admission only - cannot apply again in subsequent years. Family annual income from all sources must not exceed ₹2,50,000. Student must be enrolled in regular full-time courses in government or government-aided high schools and higher secondary schools within Odisha state. Private school students are NOT eligible. Students already receiving other ST scholarships like Post-Matric ST remain eligible for this scheme as it can be combined. Selection based on eligibility verification - all eligible ST students who meet criteria will receive scholarship (not merit-based competition). Application window opens twice yearly - applications invited during July-August and December-January cycles. School Principal must validate application - individual student applications not accepted.
intro_seo: The Shahid Madho Singh Haath Kharcha Yojana provides one-time financial incentive of ₹5,000 to Scheduled Tribe students in Odisha joining Class 9 after completing Class 8 or joining Class 11 after completing Class 10 in government and government-aided schools with family income below ₹2.5 lakh annually to reduce dropout rates and encourage ST students to continue their secondary and higher secondary education without financial barriers.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, UG, PG, Professional courses)
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Complete reimbursement of tuition fees, admission fees, and examination fees paid to the institution and Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA). Maintenance Allowance: Rs 7,000 per year for day scholar students (not residing in government hostels). Rs 13,500 per year for hosteller students (residing in government hostels). Hostel Fee Support: Reimbursement of hostel fees for students in both government and private hostels (subject to verification). Counselling Support: 2/3rd of counselling fee reimbursement for students in counselling-based admission courses. Direct Benefit Transfer: All scholarship amounts credited directly to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT. Additional Benefits: 30% quota reserved for female students. Freeship Card available for download after completing Aadhaar e-KYC. No need to visit multiple offices - fully online process.
docs_needed:
SC Caste Certificate
Income Certificate (annual family income <= Rs 2.5 lakh)
Previous year mark sheets (minimum 50% marks)
Admission receipt/fee receipt
Aadhaar card
Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked)
Domicile certificate/proof of Karnataka residency
Academic records from UUCMS portal or university
NSP One Time Registration (OTR) ID - MANDATORY
For hostellers: Registration in State Hostel Portal (SHP) or SSP-HMIS portal
deadline: January 15, 2026
step_guide: Step 1: Complete NSP One Time Registration (MANDATORY FIRST STEP) - Visit scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal). Click 'One Time Registration (OTR)'. Complete Aadhaar-based eKYC and Face Authentication using NSP OTR App. Receive your OTR Number - REQUIRED for SSP application. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name matching Aadhaar, mobile number, email ID. Verify OTP sent to mobile. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal with credentials. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Social Welfare Department - SC Category'. Enter NSP OTR ID (from Step 1). Fill personal details (name, date of birth, gender, contact details). Enter academic information (course, institution, year, previous exam marks). Provide family details (father/mother name, occupation, annual income). Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked account mandatory). Answer domicile question (Are you resident of Karnataka? Yes). For hostellers: Select 'Hosteller' ONLY if in government-run hostel (private hostel/PG = day scholar). Step 4: Upload Documents - Upload clear scanned copies of all required documents (see 'Documents Needed' section). Ensure file format and size requirements are met. Documents must be legible and complete. Step 5: Submit Application - Preview filled application carefully. Check all details for accuracy. Tick declaration checkbox confirming information is true. level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, General Degree, Professional/Technical Courses, UG, PG). NOT applicable for correspondence/distance learning or certificate/training programs.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Complete reimbursement of tuition fees and admission fees. Full payment of fees paid to recognized government or private institutions. Family Limit: Maximum two children per family eligible across all Ministry of Minority Affairs scholarships. NOT Eligible For: Correspondence or distance learning courses. Certificate or training programs. Students receiving other central government-funded scholarships.

step_guide: Step 1: NSP One Time Registration (MANDATORY FIRST PORTAL) - Visit https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal). Click 'One Time Registration (OTR)'. Download NSP OTR Mobile App for Face Authentication. Complete Aadhaar-based eKYC using Face Authentication in app. Receive your OTR Number - this is CRITICAL for both NSP and SSP applications. Step 2: Apply on NSP (FIRST APPLICATION) - Log in to NSP with OTR credentials. Select 'Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities' scheme. Select Karnataka as your state. Choose PMS (general courses, income <= Rs 2L) OR MCM (professional courses, income <= Rs 2.5L). Fill application form with personal, academic, family, bank details. Upload all required documents on NSP portal. Submit NSP application. Note your NSP Application ID - REQUIRED for SSP application. Step 3: Apply on SSP (SECOND APPLICATION) - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Create account or log in. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Minority Welfare Department'. Enter your NSP Application ID from Step 2 (MANDATORY field). Select minority community (Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi). Fill SSP application form. Upload documents again on SSP (may require re-upload). Confirm domicile status (Karnataka resident? Yes). Step 4: E-Attestation (SSP Specific) - Visit SSP e-attestation portal separately. Upload documents for e-attestation. Visit your designated e-attestation officer IN PERSON with ORIGINAL documents. Officer will verify and e-attest your documents online. Note: E-attestation NOT required for PUC, ITI, GTTC, Diploma in Polytechnic/Education/Nursing/Pharmacy/Paramedical students. Step 5: Submit Hard Copies - Prepare TWO sets of complete application with all documents. Submit ONE full set to your college/institution principal. Keep ONE set for personal reference. Principal will forward application as per workflow. Step 6: Track Status - NSP Portal: Log in to check NSP application status. SSP Portal: Log in to check SSP application status and payment updates. Monitor both portals regularly. Address any document queries immediately.
selection: Selection based on: Two-Portal Processing: Initial screening on NSP (National Scholarship Portal) by Ministry of Minority Affairs. Secondary processing on SSP (State Scholarship Portal) by Karnataka Directorate of Minorities. Only students NOT fully covered under NSP will receive benefits under SSP. Eligibility Verification: Karnataka domicile status verified (students studying outside Karnataka also eligible if domicile). Minority community certificate validation (Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi). Income verification (PMS: <= Rs 2 lakh, MCM: <= Rs 2.5 lakh). Academic Performance: Minimum 50% marks in previous semester/year examination. Continuous academic progression required. Family Limit Check: Maximum two children per family across ALL Ministry of Minority Affairs scholarships. System checks for siblings already receiving minority scholarships. Other Scholarship Check: Students receiving other central government-funded scholarships are disqualified. Institutional Validation: Student enrollment verified by college/institution. E-attestation by designated officer (except PUC/ITI/Diploma students). Principal forwards application online. Document Verification: All certificates validated for authenticity. Aadhaar validation and NPCI bank mapping checked. Priority/Workflow: NSP processed by Government of India level. SSP processes students not covered under NSP. Budget allocation determines final numbers. Disbursement: DBT to Aadhaar-linked bank accounts with NPCI mapping.
renewal: Yes - renewable if student: Secures >= 50% marks in exams. Continues in same course and institution. Maintains income eligibility. Does not receive other central govt scholarships. Fresh application required on both NSP and SSP each year.
helpline: Directorate of Minorities: 20th Floor, V.V Towers, Dr. B.R Ambedkar Veedhi, Bangalore - 560001. Email: [email protected]. SSP Helpline: 1902. Email: [email protected]. NSP Helpline: Check scholarships.gov.in for contact.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to students from minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) pursuing education after Class 10. This scholarship has two components: PMS (Post-Matric Scholarship) for general courses with income limit Rs 2 lakh, and MCM (Merit-Cum-Means) for professional/technical courses with income limit Rs 2.5 lakh. Students must apply through BOTH National Scholarship Portal (NSP) first, then State Scholarship Portal (SSP). Karnataka domicile students studying outside the state are also eligible.

Fill enrollment form with personal details and disability information. Upload disability certificate and medical documents. Submit for verification. Receive UDID number and card (may take time for processing). IMPORTANT: Complete this BEFORE SSP scholarship application if you don't have UDID. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name, mobile, email. Verify OTP. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens' OR 'Disability Welfare Department'. Fill personal details (name, DOB, gender, contact). Answer domicile question: 'Are you a resident of Karnataka?' - Select YES. Step 4: Enter UDID Information (CRITICAL STEP) - When prompted for disability information, select 'Yes' for 'Do you have disability?'. Enter your UDID number in the designated field. Click 'Get Data' or 'Fetch Details' button. System will automatically populate: Disability type (locomotor/visual/hearing/intellectual/etc.). Disability percentage (e.g., 40%, 60%, 80%). This data comes directly from central UDID database - verify it appears correctly. Step 5: Complete Application - Fill academic information (course, institution, previous exam marks >= 50%). Enter family details (parent names, occupation, annual income). Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked account). Upload required documents (see 'Documents Needed' section). IMPORTANT: For hosteller status, select 'Hosteller' ONLY if living in government-run hostel. Private hostel/PG accommodation = Day Scholar. Step 6: Submit Application - Preview application carefully. Verify UDID data auto-populated correctly. Verify all disability information is accurate. Check declaration checkbox. Click 'Final Submit'. Download acknowledgment slip and note Application Reference Number. Step 7: Institutional Verification - Submit acknowledgment slip to your college/school office. Submit UDID card copy and other required documents to institution. College principal/head will verify application online. Track verification status in your SSP login. Step 8: Track Application Status - Log in to SSP portal regularly. Check application status (Submitted/Under Verification/Approved/Rejected). If status shows 'Invalid Document', address issues immediately before deadline. Monitor payment status once approved.
selection: Selection based on: UDID Validation: System verifies UDID number with central database (swavlambancard.gov.in). UDID must be active and valid. Disability type and percentage cross-checked automatically. Students without valid UDID are ineligible. Eligibility Verification: Karnataka domicile status verified through certificates. Income verification (limit not clearly specified - likely <= Rs 2.5 lakh). Caste verification if applicable (SC/ST/OBC/Minority with disability). Academic Performance: Minimum 50% marks in previous examination mandatory. Academic progression and enrollment verified. Institutional Validation: Student enrollment in recognized Karnataka institution verified. College principal/head approval required. Academic records cross-checked. Document Verification: All uploaded documents validated. Aadhaar and bank account seeding verified. UDID card and disability certificate authenticity checked. Priority/Consideration: Differently-abled students across all caste categories eligible. Extended deadline (Dec 31) allows more time for disabled students to complete applications. Processing: Applications processed by Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka. Budget allocation for disability welfare determines approvals. Disbursement: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Payment timeline typically February-July after final approval.
renewal: Yes - renewable annually. Renewal conditions: Maintain minimum 50% marks each year. Continue meeting income eligibility. UDID card must remain valid. Fresh application required each academic year through SSP portal.
helpline: SSP Helpline: 1902. Email: [email protected]. Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka (for disability-specific queries). UDID Helpline: Check https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in for UDID card related queries.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to differently-abled students pursuing education after Class 10. Administered by the Department for the Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens through the State Scholarship Portal (SSP), this scholarship requires students to possess a valid UDID (Unique Disability ID) card. The system automatically fetches disability type and percentage from the central UDID database. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents with minimum 50% marks in previous exam.

This is central scheme parameter."},{"question":"Can I study outside Karnataka?","answer":"Karnataka domicile students studying in Classes 1-8 in schools outside Karnataka are also eligible, but must be Karnataka residents."},{"question":"Do I need NSP registration for Pre-Matric?","answer":"No, NSP (National Scholarship Portal) registration is NOT required for Pre-Matric Minorities. NSP is only mandatory for Post-Matric Minorities."}]

title: Pre-Matric PWD/UDID (Karnataka)
caste: ["Students with Disabilities (Persons with Disabilities - PWD) with valid UDID"]
benefits: Financial assistance for students with disabilities registered under UDID (Unique Disability ID) system. CRITICAL FEATURE: Enter UDID number in application → disability type and percentage auto-populate from central database (no manual disability certificate upload needed). Covers tuition fees and maintenance. 75% attendance MANDATORY throughout academic year. Scholarship amount varies by disability type/percentage and class level. Administered by Disability Welfare Department. DBT payment to Aadhaar-linked account. UDID registration is PREREQUISITE - students without UDID must first register at https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in before applying for scholarship.
docs_needed:
(1) SATS ID
(2) Aadhaar card linked to bank account
(3) Valid UDID (Unique Disability ID) card/number - MANDATORY
(4) Income certificate
(5) Previous year marks card (not for Class 1)
(6) Bank passbook (Aadhaar-seeded
NPCI-mapped)
(7) Passport-size photo
(8) School bonafide certificate
(9) Disability certificate (percentage of disability auto-fetched from UDID database)
deadline: December 31, 2025 (Disability Welfare Department typically has extended deadline)
step_guide: (1) FIRST: Obtain UDID if not already registered at https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in; (2) Obtain SATS ID from school; (3) Visit https://ssp.karnataka.gov.in Pre-Matric section; (4) Create account with SATS ID, Aadhaar, mobile, email; (5) Login with credentials; (6) Complete e-KYC; (7) Fill application form; (8) Enter UDID number - disability details will auto-populate; (9) Upload other documents (income certificate, marks card, bank passbook); (10) Verify auto-fetched disability information matches UDID card; (11) Submit application; (12) Save acknowledgment; (13) Track status via portal
selection: Processed by Disability Welfare Department: (1) Online application submission; (2) UDID validation - disability type/percentage auto-fetched from central UDID database; (3) School enrollment verification; (4) Income verification; (5) Academic performance check (50% marks, 75% attendance); (6) Aadhaar and bank validation; (7) Final approval by Disability Welfare Department; (8) DBT disbursement. UDID system eliminates need for manual disability certificate verification. All eligible students with valid UDID receive scholarship.
renewal: Annual renewal with auto-processing for previous beneficiaries. Must maintain 50% marks and 75% attendance. UDID must remain valid. Income verification required annually.
helpline: Disability Welfare Department helpline (check portal); UDID helpline: Check swavlambancard.gov.in; SSP support: 080-35254757
intro_seo: Karnataka's Pre-Matric PWD scholarship supports students with disabilities in Classes 1-10 using UDID system for automatic disability verification and providing financial assistance for education.
faq_json: [{"question":"What is UDID and how do I get it?","answer":"UDID (Unique Disability ID) is a central government ID for persons with disabilities. Register at https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in to obtain UDID card. This is MANDATORY before applying for PWD scholarship."},{"question":"Do I need to upload disability certificate?","answer":"No, enter your UDID number in application and disability type/percentage will automatically populate from UDID database. Manual disability certificate upload not required."},{"question":"What is the attendance requirement?","answer":"PWD students must maintain 75% attendance throughout the academic year. This is strictly monitored for scholarship eligibility."},{"question":"Can students with any disability apply?","answer":"Yes, students with any recognized disability registered in UDID system are eligible. Disability percentage is auto-fetched from UDID."},{"question":"Is there a separate income limit for PWD?","answer":"Income limit typically ₹2.5 lakh per annum, but verify on portal as it may vary. Generally aligned with SC/ST Classes 9-10 limit."}]
